addKey: Connect OBD tool (Xhorse VVDI2, Autel IM608, AK90+) to port under driver dash (left of column). Select BMW > EWS/CAS. For EWS2/3/4 add-key: read EEPROM via OBD or clip adapter on EWS PCB, select empty key slot, generate dealer key with transponder programmer. Engine ISN synced automatically. No PIN required.
allKeysLost: Bench/EEPROM required. Remove EWS module (behind dash or under bonnet depending on gen). Read 95040/95080 EEPROM with AK90+, BMW Multi-Tool, or Xhorse VVDI Prog. Extract key array, generate new transponder, write back. Resync DME ISN via INPA or equivalent after.
obdPort: Driver footwell, left of steering column (E36: engine bay on very early cars; E46 post-2000: under trim flap right of column)
specialTool: AK90+ or Xhorse VVDI Prog (EEPROM/bench for AKL); Autel IM608 / VVDI2 (OBD add-key)

addKey: Connect Xhorse VVDI2, Autel IM508/IM608, or CGDI BMW to OBD port (driver footwell, left of column). Navigate BMW > CAS Key Learn > CAS2 or CAS3/CAS3+. With working key in ignition (position II), read key info, enable empty slot, generate dealer key on programmer, test start. WiFi/server connection needed for Autel. No PIN required for add-key via OBD.
allKeysLost: CAS2: bench EEPROM preferred (R270 or VVDI Prog chip read). CAS3/CAS3+: OBD AKL possible with VVDI Key Tool Plus or CGDI BMW (activate ignition stub by pressing brake + lights); requires ISN from DME. Bench is safer: read 95128 EEPROM, modify key table, write back, then resync via OBD.
pinSource: ISN/CS read from DME via OBD (for AKL) or EEPROM dump
obdPort: Driver footwell, left of steering column, behind small door in lower kick panel
specialTool: Xhorse VVDI2 / Autel IM608 / CGDI BMW (OBD add-key); VVDI Prog or R270 (bench AKL)

addKey: Connect VVDI2 / Autel IM608 / CGDI BMW / Abrites AVDI+BN016 to OBD port. Select BMW > FEM Key Learn. With working key in ignition, read ISN from DME (BN012 license on Abrites or VVDI Prog), enable empty key slot, generate dealer key, code to vehicle. Requires extra power supply (13V+). BDC (later F30 2015+) by OBD up to software v85; higher versions require bench unlock first.
allKeysLost: Bench EEPROM/bench required for most. Remove FEM/BDC module (A-pillar passenger footwell). Read 95256 chip with VVDI Prog or Yanhua ACDP. Read ISN from DME/DDE via bench. Write modified EEPROM back. Restore factory coding via OBD. Generate and code new dealer key. Abrites BN013/BN016 + BN012 support this workflow.
pinSource: ISN/CS read from DME via bench or VVDI Prog
obdPort: Driver footwell, left of steering column
specialTool: VVDI2 + VVDI Prog (EEPROM/bench); Autel IM608 Pro; Abrites AVDI + BN013/BN016 + BN012; Yanhua ACDP

addKey: OBD with BDC2-capable tool (CGDI A2, Xhorse VVDI2 + BDC2 adapter, Autel IM608 Pro, Abrites BN020). Navigate BMW > BDC2 Immo > Add Key. With one working key present, ISN can typically be read via OBD from DME. Requires stable power supply (13V+).
allKeysLost: Bench EEPROM/bench required. Remove BDC2 module. Read SPC5777 processor data via BDC2 bench platform (Yanhua ACDP Module 38, Xhorse BDC2 adapter + Bosch adapter). Read ISN from B48 DME via bench. Backup FAFP data, write IMMO data, write coding, generate dealer key, write DME ISN, reset EGS EWS. Xhorse BDC2 AKL released May 2026; hybrid models not supported.
pinSource: ISN read from B48 DME via bench (not OBD)
obdPort: Driver footwell, left of steering column
specialTool: CGDI A2; Xhorse VVDI2 + BDC2 adapter + Bosch adapter; Autel IM608 Pro (per-VIN fee); Yanhua ACDP2 + Module 38 (EEPROM/bench for AKL)

addKey: Same procedure as F30 3 Series (FEM platform). Connect VVDI2 / Autel IM608 / CGDI BMW to OBD port. Read ISN from DME, enable key slot, generate dealer key, code to vehicle with working key in ignition. Requires extra power supply.
allKeysLost: Same bench EEPROM procedure as F30. Remove FEM/BDC module (passenger footwell A-pillar). Read 95256 chip, extract ISN from DME on bench, write back, generate and code new key. CGDI BMW or Abrites BN013+BN016+BN012 or VVDI2+VVDI Prog.
pinSource: ISN/CS read from DME via bench
obdPort: Driver footwell, left of steering column
specialTool: VVDI2 + VVDI Prog (EEPROM/bench); Autel IM608; Abrites AVDI + BN013/BN016 + BN012

addKey: Same as E90: OBD with VVDI2 / Autel IM608 / CGDI BMW. CAS2 (2004-2006) or CAS3 (2007) or CAS3+ (2008-2010). Connect to driver footwell OBD port. Select CAS version, read key info with working key, enable slot, generate dealer key. No PIN for add-key.
allKeysLost: CAS2: bench EEPROM preferred. CAS3/CAS3+: OBD AKL possible (VVDI Key Tool Plus or CGDI) with ISN from DME, or bench EEPROM for reliability. Same workflow as E90.
pinSource: ISN/CS read from DME (for AKL) or EEPROM dump
obdPort: Driver footwell, left of steering column
specialTool: Xhorse VVDI2 / Autel IM608 / CGDI BMW (OBD add-key); VVDI Prog or AK90+ (bench AKL)

addKey: OBD with VVDI2 / Autel IM608 / CGDI BMW to driver footwell port. Select BMW > CAS4/CAS4+ Key Learn. With working key in ignition (position II), unlock CAS module (OBD unlock takes ~10 min, requires 13V+ power), enable empty slot, generate dealer key, code to vehicle.
allKeysLost: CAS4+: ISN required. OBD AKL possible if ISN known (select 'Known ISN', brake+lights to wake bus). ISN source: VVDI Prog bench read from DME, or Autel EEPROM method (30 min vs 4-6 hr OBD). Bench safer: read CAS4+ EEPROM (95256), modify key table, write back, then OBD coding.
pinSource: ISN read from DME via VVDI Prog bench or Autel EEPROM
obdPort: Driver footwell, left of steering column
specialTool: Xhorse VVDI2 + VVDI Prog (EEPROM/bench for AKL); Autel IM608 Pro; CGDI BMW

addKey: OBD with BDC2-capable tool (CGDI A2, Xhorse VVDI2 + BDC2 adapter, Autel IM608 Pro). Same as G20 3 Series: navigate BDC2 Immo > Add Key. Working key required; ISN read from DME via OBD during add-key.
allKeysLost: Bench required. Same BDC2 AKL procedure as G20: bench platform + BDC2 adapter, read SPC5777, read ISN from B48 DME on bench. Yanhua ACDP2 Module 38 or Xhorse BDC2 adapter + Bosch adapter. Xhorse BDC2 AKL supported from May 2026.
pinSource: ISN read from B48 DME via bench
obdPort: Driver footwell, left of steering column
specialTool: CGDI A2; Xhorse VVDI2 + BDC2 + Bosch adapter; Yanhua ACDP2 + Module 38 (EEPROM/bench for AKL)

addKey: Same as E90 CAS3+ procedure. Connect VVDI2 / Autel IM608 to driver footwell OBD port. Select CAS3+, enable empty key slot with working key in ignition, generate dealer key on programmer.
allKeysLost: CAS3+ OBD AKL (VVDI Key Tool Plus or CGDI BMW) with ISN from DME, or bench EEPROM. Same procedure as E90 CAS3+.
pinSource: ISN read from DME (for AKL)
obdPort: Driver footwell, left of steering column
specialTool: Xhorse VVDI2 / Autel IM608 / CGDI BMW

addKey: FEM/BDC platform. Connect VVDI2 / Autel IM608 / CGDI BMW to driver footwell OBD port. Select FEM Key Learn. With working key in ignition, read ISN from DME, enable slot, generate and code dealer key. 13V+ power supply needed.
allKeysLost: Bench EEPROM for FEM: remove FEM module (passenger footwell A-pillar), read 95256 chip, extract ISN from DME on bench, write back, code new key via OBD. BDC (later build dates): same bench workflow or OBD if BDC version ≤85.
pinSource: ISN read from DME via bench
obdPort: Driver footwell, left of steering column
specialTool: VVDI2 + VVDI Prog (EEPROM/bench); Autel IM608; CGDI BMW; Abrites BN016

addKey: EWS3/EWS4 platform. OBD with Xhorse VVDI2 / Autel IM608 / AK90+. Connect to driver footwell OBD port. Select EWS, read EEPROM or use OBD key learn, generate dealer key on transponder programmer.
allKeysLost: Bench EEPROM: remove EWS module, read EEPROM with AK90+ or VVDI Prog, rebuild key table, write back. Resync DME via INPA or OBD tool.
obdPort: Driver footwell, left of steering column
specialTool: AK90+ or VVDI Prog (EEPROM/bench for AKL); Autel IM608 / VVDI2 (OBD add-key)

addKey: CAS4 platform (not FEM). OBD with VVDI2 / Autel IM608 / CGDI BMW. Same procedure as F10 5-series CAS4: unlock CAS, enable key slot with working key, generate dealer key. 13V+ required.
allKeysLost: CAS4 AKL: ISN from DME bench read or OBD (if ISN known). Bench EEPROM safer. Read CAS4 95256 chip, read ISN from DME, write back EEPROM, generate and code new key.
pinSource: ISN read from DME via bench or VVDI Prog
obdPort: Driver footwell, left of steering column
specialTool: Xhorse VVDI2 + VVDI Prog (EEPROM/bench for AKL); Autel IM608; CGDI BMW

addKey: BDC2 platform. Same as G20/G30: OBD with CGDI A2, Xhorse VVDI2 + BDC2 adapter, or Autel IM608 Pro. Working key required for OBD add-key.
allKeysLost: Bench required. Remove BDC2 module, use BDC2 bench platform (Yanhua ACDP2 Module 38 or Xhorse BDC2 adapter). Read SPC5777, read ISN from DME on bench. Same G-series BDC2 AKL workflow.
pinSource: ISN read from DME via bench
obdPort: Driver footwell, left of steering column
specialTool: CGDI A2; Xhorse VVDI2 + BDC2 adapter; Yanhua ACDP2 + Module 38 (EEPROM/bench for AKL)

addKey: EWS3/EWS4 platform. OBD with VVDI2 / Autel IM608 / AK90+. Select EWS system, read EEPROM or OBD key learn, generate dealer key on transponder programmer. Driver footwell OBD port.
allKeysLost: Bench EEPROM: remove EWS module, read 93C86/95080 EEPROM with AK90+ or VVDI Prog, rebuild key table, write back. Resync DME.
obdPort: Driver footwell, left of steering column
specialTool: AK90+ or VVDI Prog (EEPROM/bench for AKL); Autel IM608 / VVDI2 (OBD add-key)

addKey: CAS3/CAS3+ platform. Connect VVDI2 / Autel IM608 / CGDI BMW to driver footwell OBD port. Select CAS3 or CAS3+. With working key in ignition, read key info, enable empty slot, generate dealer key, test start.
allKeysLost: CAS3+ AKL: OBD possible with VVDI Key Tool Plus or CGDI BMW (ISN from DME required); bench EEPROM recommended as safer. Read CAS3+ 95128 chip on bench, modify key table, write back, generate new key.
pinSource: ISN read from DME (for AKL) or EEPROM bench dump
obdPort: Driver footwell, left of steering column
specialTool: Xhorse VVDI2 / CGDI BMW (OBD CAS3+); VVDI Prog or AK90+ (bench AKL)

addKey: FEM/BDC platform. Same as F30/F10: OBD with VVDI2 / Autel IM608 / CGDI BMW. Select FEM Key Learn, working key in ignition, read ISN, enable slot, generate and code dealer key. 13V+ power supply.
allKeysLost: Bench EEPROM: remove FEM/BDC from passenger footwell A-pillar, read 95256 chip, extract ISN from DME on bench, write back, code new key via OBD. BDC v85+ requires bench unlock before OBD.
pinSource: ISN read from DME via bench
obdPort: Driver footwell, left of steering column
specialTool: VVDI2 + VVDI Prog (EEPROM/bench); Autel IM608; CGDI BMW; Abrites BN016

addKey: BDC2 platform. OBD with CGDI A2 / Xhorse VVDI2 + BDC2 adapter / Autel IM608 Pro. Working key required. ISN from DME via OBD during add-key flow.
allKeysLost: Bench required. BDC2 bench platform (Yanhua ACDP2 Module 38 or Xhorse BDC2 + Bosch adapter). Read SPC5777, read B48 DME ISN on bench. Xhorse BDC2 AKL supported May 2026; Autel requires per-VIN fee.
pinSource: ISN read from B48 DME via bench
obdPort: Driver footwell, left of steering column
specialTool: CGDI A2; Xhorse VVDI2 + BDC2 + Bosch adapter; Yanhua ACDP2 + Module 38 (EEPROM/bench for AKL)

addKey: CAS3/CAS3+ platform (BMW architecture). Connect VVDI2 / Autel IM608 / CGDI BMW to driver footwell OBD port. Select CAS3 or CAS3+. With working key present, enable empty slot, generate dealer key, code to vehicle.
allKeysLost: CAS3/CAS3+ AKL: bench EEPROM recommended. Remove CAS module from passenger footwell, read 95128 chip with VVDI Prog or Xprog, rebuild key table, write back. Alternatively VVDI Key Tool Plus via OBD if ISN known. Resync DME afterward.
pinSource: ISN read from DME (for AKL) or EEPROM bench dump
obdPort: Driver footwell, left of steering column
specialTool: Xhorse VVDI2 / CGDI BMW / Autel IM608 (OBD add-key); VVDI Prog or Xprog (bench AKL)

addKey: FEM/BDC platform (BMW architecture). Connect VVDI2 / Autel IM608 / CGDI BMW / Abrites BN016 to driver footwell OBD port. FEM module in passenger footwell A-pillar. With working key in ignition, read ISN from DME, enable key slot, generate and code dealer key. 13V+ power supply required.
allKeysLost: Bench EEPROM: remove FEM/BDC module, read 95256 chip, extract ISN from DME on bench, write back, generate and code new key via OBD. BDC v85+ requires bench unlock. Mail-in bench services available.
pinSource: ISN read from DME via bench
obdPort: Driver footwell, left of steering column
specialTool: VVDI2 + VVDI Prog (EEPROM/bench); Autel IM608; CGDI BMW; Abrites BN016 + BN012 (EEPROM/bench for AKL)

model: C-Class / E-Class / S-Class / GLA / GLC / GLE (FBS3 era)
addKey: FBS3: connect CGDI MB / Xhorse VVDI MB / Autel IM608 to OBD port (driver footwell, left of column). Read EIS/EZS module — tool interrogates the Electronic Ignition Switch to extract encrypted key data and calculates PIN/SKC. If working key available, EIS enters learning mode allowing key addition without full EEPROM extraction. Cut HU64 blade to bitting, code key shell to vehicle. Locksmith-capable job.
allKeysLost: Remove EIS from column, read NEC processor directly (EEPROM/bench via CGDI MB or VVDI MB EIS adapter), extract key data, generate new NEC key. Alternatively some tools can do full AKL via OBD. Cut blade, program transponder, register to EIS. Full on-site possible same-day for experienced locksmith.
pinSource: Password calc from EIS via CGDI MB / VVDI MB / Autel IM608 OBD read; or EEPROM/bench dump of EIS NEC processor
obdPort: Driver footwell, left of steering column (under small panel near hood release)
specialTool: CGDI MB; Xhorse VVDI MB BGA Tool; Autel IM608; Abrites AVDI + AB012

model: C-Class (W205) / E-Class (W213) / A-Class (W177) / GLA (X247) / GLC (X253) / GLE (W167) / S-Class (W222) (FBS4 era)
addKey: DEALER ONLY. FBS4 requires online authentication via encrypted Mercedes-Benz factory servers (Germany). Programming demands genuine XENTRY software with a high-authorization FBS4 online dealer account plus an MB Star C5/C6 or SD Connect C4. No aftermarket tool (CGDI MB, VVDI MB, Autel, Abrites) can perform FBS4 key addition. Locksmith scope: diagnostics, key-cut only (if bitting on file), and physical lockout assistance.
allKeysLost: Dealer/tow only. Mercedes-Benz dealer with XENTRY online access. No independent locksmith AKL option for FBS4.
pinSource: dealer-only (Daimler FBS4 server — not accessible to aftermarket)
obdPort: Driver footwell, left of steering column
specialTool: Mercedes XENTRY (dealer-only)

addKey: DEALER ONLY. W176 A-Class US market uses FBS4 from production start (~2013). Same restriction as W205/W213: requires XENTRY with FBS4 dealer server access. Locksmith scope = lockout only.
allKeysLost: Dealer/tow only. No aftermarket AKL for FBS4.
pinSource: dealer-only (Daimler FBS4 server)
obdPort: Driver footwell, left of steering column
specialTool: Mercedes XENTRY (dealer-only)

addKey: CEM PIN-based. Connect Autel IM608 / Lonsdor K518 / Yanhua ACDP (Module 12) to OBD port (above parking-brake release, under headlight switch). Read CEM PIN via OBD (up to 8 hours) or ICP bench method (~30 min, preferred). Once PIN decoded, use it to authorize key coding via OBD: select Volvo > IMMO > P2 > Add Key, provide saved CEM data, place new key in induction area/cup holder, confirm.
allKeysLost: CEM bench read required. Remove CEM (under co-pilot airbag). Use OBP+ICP adapter + Yanhua ACDP or Lonsdor K518 CEM adapter to read CEM security data without soldering. After CEM data read, proceed with add-key OBD flow as above. No working key needed once CEM data is extracted.
pinSource: CEM PIN decoded via OBD (VDD/ModUnlock/Autel — up to 8 hr) or ICP bench read (~30 min); CEM security data file saved locally
obdPort: Above parking-brake release, below headlight switch (driver side dash area)
securityWait: CEM PIN decode: 30 min (ICP bench) to 8 hours (OBD); stable power supply required throughout
specialTool: Yanhua ACDP + Module 12 + OBP+ICP adapter (ICP bench preferred); Lonsdor K518 with Volvo license; Autel IM608 Pro

addKey: SPA/CEM platform. Connect Lonsdor K518 (Volvo license) / Yanhua ACDP Module 20 / Autel IM608 Pro to OBD port (above parking-brake release). Select Volvo > IMMO > XC90 (2016+). Add-key: read CEM data by ICP (disassemble CEM, install OBP+ICP adapter, connect interface board) or OBD (4-6 hr decrypt). Once CEM data read, key add via OBD: select model, provide CEM file, place new HU101 key in cup holder, confirm.
allKeysLost: Bench CEM read required (ICP method ~30 min vs OBD ~4-6 hr). Remove CEM (under driver steering wheel area). Yanhua ACDP Module 20: install interface board + 20-pin cable, select SPC5646C or SPC5748G chip, read CEM data. Then program key via OBD. Lonsdor K518 and Autel IM608 Pro also support with CEM adapter.
pinSource: CEM PIN/security data decoded from CEM module via ICP bench read (Yanhua ACDP Module 20) or OBD decrypt (Lonsdor/Autel — 4-6 hr)
obdPort: Above parking-brake release, below headlight switch (driver side)
securityWait: ICP bench: ~30 min; OBD decrypt: 4-6 hours; continuous power supply required
specialTool: Yanhua ACDP + Module 20 + OBP+ICP adapter (EEPROM/bench preferred); Lonsdor K518 with Volvo license; Autel IM608 Pro

addKey: P3 CEM platform. Same procedure as XC90 P2 but P3: connect Autel IM608 / Lonsdor K518 / Yanhua ACDP (Module 12) to OBD port. Read CEM PIN via ICP bench (~30 min, recommended) or OBD (up to 8 hr). Add key via OBD using saved CEM data file. HU56 blade key or remote head key placed in induction zone.
allKeysLost: CEM bench ICP read required. Remove CEM (under co-pilot airbag). ACDP OBP+ICP adapter reads CEM without soldering. Then proceed with OBD add-key flow using CEM data. 2017 P3 models may have restrictions — verify tool compatibility.
pinSource: CEM security data decoded via ICP bench read (Yanhua ACDP Module 12) or OBD decrypt (up to 8 hr)
obdPort: Above parking-brake release, below headlight switch (driver side)
securityWait: ICP bench: ~30 min; OBD decrypt: up to 8 hours
specialTool: Yanhua ACDP + Module 12 + OBP+ICP adapter; Lonsdor K518 with Volvo license; Autel IM608

addKey: SPA CEM platform, identical to XC90 second gen. Lonsdor K518 (Volvo license) / Yanhua ACDP Module 20 / Autel IM608 Pro via OBD. Read CEM data ICP bench (~30 min) or OBD (~4-6 hr). Add key via OBD with CEM data file, HU101 key in cup holder.
allKeysLost: Bench CEM read required (ICP, ~30 min). Remove CEM, use Yanhua ACDP Module 20 ICP interface. Then OBD key learn flow with extracted data.
pinSource: CEM PIN/security data via ICP bench (Yanhua ACDP Module 20) or OBD decrypt (Lonsdor/Autel)
obdPort: Above parking-brake release, below headlight switch (driver side)
specialTool: Yanhua ACDP + Module 20 (EEPROM/bench preferred); Lonsdor K518; Autel IM608 Pro

addKey: P3 CEM platform. Same as XC60 first gen / XC90 P2 P3 procedure: Yanhua ACDP Module 12 / Lonsdor K518 / Autel IM608. ICP bench CEM read (~30 min) preferred, then OBD key add. HU56 remote head key.
allKeysLost: CEM ICP bench read required. Remove CEM, use ACDP OBP+ICP adapter. Extract CEM data, proceed with OBD add-key.
pinSource: CEM security data via ICP bench or OBD decrypt
obdPort: Above parking-brake release, below headlight switch (driver side)
securityWait: ICP bench: ~30 min; OBD: up to 8 hours
specialTool: Yanhua ACDP + Module 12 + OBP+ICP adapter; Lonsdor K518; Autel IM608

addKey: SPA CEM platform, same as XC60 second gen / XC90 second gen. Lonsdor K518 / Yanhua ACDP Module 20 / Autel IM608 Pro. ICP bench CEM read (~30 min) or OBD (~4-6 hr). HU101 key add via OBD after CEM data extracted.
allKeysLost: Bench CEM ICP read (Yanhua ACDP Module 20), then OBD key learn. No working key needed after CEM data obtained.
pinSource: CEM PIN/security data via ICP bench (Yanhua ACDP Module 20) or OBD decrypt
obdPort: Above parking-brake release, below headlight switch (driver side)
specialTool: Yanhua ACDP + Module 20 (EEPROM/bench preferred); Lonsdor K518; Autel IM608 Pro

onboard: REMOTE ONLY (not transponder). Requires 2 already-programmed working remotes. Insert programmed key, turn ignition ON 5 sec, OFF, remove. Within 30 sec insert second programmed key, turn ON — IMMO light blinks = program mode. For remote: press UNLOCK on working remote 4-10 sec, hold LOCK; within 10 sec release both. Then press LOCK+UNLOCK together 2 sec, release 1 sec, press UNLOCK on new remote to program. NOTE: transponder chip must still be programmed by OBD tool separately. Early 2007-2009 years use jumper-wire door-lock method. 2016+ models NOT onboard-programmable.
addKey: Connect Autel IM608 / Lonsdor K518 / Xhorse VVDI Key Tool Plus to OBD port (driver footwell, above brake pedal). Select Mitsubishi > Outlander > model year. Read key data, program new ID46 transponder key in slot. Remote portion may be added via onboard sequence (if 2 working remotes available) or via OBD tool simultaneously. No PIN required for most 2007-2018 ID46 models.
allKeysLost: OBD with Autel IM608 / OBDSTAR X300 DP. Tool extracts PIN from BCM/ECU via OBD. No bench typically needed for ID46 era. Dealer can retrieve PIN from Mitsubishi database via VIN if tool cannot extract.
pinSource: OBD extraction via Autel/OBDSTAR; or dealer database via VIN (if needed for AKL)
obdPort: Driver footwell, under dash above brake pedal
specialTool: Autel IM608 / Lonsdor K518 / OBDSTAR X300 DP (OBD)

onboard: REMOTE ONLY (same as Outlander). Requires 2 working programmed remotes. Transponder must be programmed via OBD tool. Not all years/trims support onboard remote procedure — verify per year.
addKey: Connect Autel IM608 / Lonsdor K518 / OBDSTAR X300 DP to OBD port (driver footwell, above brake pedal). Select Mitsubishi > Outlander Sport > year. Program new ID46 transponder key. Remote portion via onboard if 2 working remotes available.
allKeysLost: OBD with Autel IM608 or OBDSTAR. Tool extracts security data. No bench required for ID46 era. Dealer via VIN as fallback.
pinSource: OBD extraction; dealer database via VIN as fallback
obdPort: Driver footwell, under dash above brake pedal
specialTool: Autel IM608 / Lonsdor K518 / OBDSTAR X300 DP

addKey: OBD with Autel IM608 / Xhorse VVDI Key Tool Plus to OBD port (driver footwell, above brake pedal). Select Mitsubishi > Mirage > year. Program new ID46 transponder. No PIN required. DIY self-programming NOT available for transponder; remote-only onboard procedure requires 2 working remotes and is model-year dependent.
allKeysLost: OBD with Autel IM608 or OBDSTAR. ID46 era — no bench needed. Tool reads security data from BCM/ECU via OBD. Dealer via VIN as fallback.
pinSource: OBD extraction; dealer database via VIN as fallback
obdPort: Driver footwell, under dash above brake pedal
specialTool: Autel IM608 / Xhorse VVDI Key Tool Plus / OBDSTAR X300 DP

addKey: ID47 (Hitag3) era. Connect Lonsdor K518 / OBDSTAR X300 DP Plus / Autel IM608 / Xhorse VVDI Key Tool Plus to OBD port (driver footwell, above brake pedal). Select Mitsubishi > Eclipse Cross > year. Program new ID47 transponder/smart key. Smart key (proximity) trims: BCM40 cable from Xhorse may be required to bypass PIN for some configurations. No onboard procedure.
allKeysLost: OBD with Lonsdor K518 / OBDSTAR X300 DP Plus / Autel IM608. ID47 era — OBD AKL supported by these tools. Smart Pro (Advanced Diagnostics) also confirmed via video for 2024 Eclipse Cross AKL. No bench required. Dealer (MUT III) as fallback.
pinSource: OBD extraction via tool (some configurations may require BCM40 cable to bypass PIN — see Xhorse VVDI Key Tool Plus + BCM40 cable)
obdPort: Driver footwell, under dash above brake pedal
specialTool: Lonsdor K518 / OBDSTAR X300 DP Plus / Autel IM608 / Xhorse VVDI Key Tool Plus (+ BCM40 cable for some trims)

onboard: App-based. Owner: Settings > Locks > Add Key on touchscreen. Place authenticated key card on center console card reader to authorize, then tap new key card/phone to reader. No mechanical key, no transponder.
addKey: Via vehicle touchscreen (Settings > Locks > Add Key Card / Phone Key / Key Fob). Requires one authenticated key card or phone key to authorize the addition. Place new key card on console card reader when prompted. Phone key: pair via Tesla app (Bluetooth + NFC). No locksmith tool applicable — locksmith scope is physical lockout only (slim jim / long reach).
allKeysLost: Tesla Service Center only. No aftermarket tool or locksmith can program Tesla keys. Vehicle must be transported to Tesla Service. Some Tesla-authorized third parties may have limited Toolbox access but key programming remains Tesla-controlled.

onboard: App-based. Identical to Model 3: Settings > Locks > Add Key on touchscreen, authenticated by existing key card on center console reader.
addKey: Via touchscreen (Settings > Locks > Add Key Card / Phone Key). Authenticate with existing key card (place on center console behind cup holder). No mechanical key, no transponder, no locksmith tool applicable. Locksmith scope = physical lockout only.
allKeysLost: Tesla Service Center only. No aftermarket AKL solution. Vehicle requires transport to Tesla Service.
